input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover current MAUI workload name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it (use the name from search output)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows App SDK / WinUI3 requires VS build tools:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or if you only want build tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required workload components:

```powershell
# From an elevated prompt — adjust path if using BuildTools
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --passive --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Install Android SDK & JDK

MAUI's workload installs some Android pieces, but you may need to manage SDKs explicitly:

```powershell
# Check what the MAUI workload already installed
dotnet android sdk list

# If you need standalone management, install Android CLI tools:
winget install Google.AndroidStudio
# ...or just the command-line tools:
# Download from https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only

# Discover available Android SDK platform versions
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;"

# Install a target API level (e.g., API 35) + build tools
sdkmanager "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
sdkmanager --licenses
```

For the JDK (MAUI requires JDK 17+):

```powershell
# Check if already present from workload install
java -version

# If missing:
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17
```

## Step 5 — Set Environment Variables

```powershell
# Discover where Android SDK landed
# (VS/MAUI default or standalone install)
$androidHome = if (Test-Path "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk") {
    "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
} elseif (Test-Path "$env:ProgramFiles\Android\android-sdk") {
    "$env:ProgramFiles\Android\android-sdk"
} else {
    Write-Warning "Android SDK not found — set ANDROID_HOME manually"
}

[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidHome, "User")
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  (Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-*" -Directory |
   Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName, "User")

# Reload in current session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME","User")
$env:JAVA_HOME    = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME","User")
```

## Step 6 — Enable Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Elevated prompt
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## Step 7 — Create & Verify a Project

```powershell
# See available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Scaffold a new app
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

## Step 8 — Android Emulator (optional)

```powershell
# List available system images
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "system-images.*google_apis"

# Install one
sdkmanager "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
avdmanager create avd -n Pixel_API35 -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Launch
emulator -avd Pixel_API35
```

---

**Key version-discovery tips:**
- `dotnet workload search` — finds current workload names (they evolve across SDK versions)
- `sdkmanager --list` — discovers available Android SDK/API versions
- `winget search <package>` — finds latest installer versions
- `dotnet new list maui` — shows templates & their target framework monikers

Adjust `net9.0-*` to `net10.0-*` etc. if you install a different SDK version. The dynamic discovery commands above will always show you what's current.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
